title: Clippy: 6 redundant closures — pass fn directly
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

**Dimension:** Cognitive Readability
**Lint:** clippy::redundant_closure

## Violation
A closure that only forwards its arg to a single function (`|x| f(x)`) is equivalent to `f` itself. The closure layer adds visual noise without adding information.

## Locations
- src/modes/mod.rs: 954
- src/pipeline.rs: 263 (twice), 268, 358
- src/tui/app.rs: 1389

## Mandated Refactor
```rust
// Before
iter.map(|x| transform(x))
// After
iter.map(transform)
```
Be careful: only drop the closure when arg types match exactly. If `f` takes `&T` and closure arg is `T`, or vice versa, the closure is NOT redundant — clippy's suggestion is safe here by construction.
